title: hydrocotyle laxiflora
intro: Hydrocotyle laxiflora, also known as Indian pennywort or Gotu Kola, is a plant native to tropical Asia, recognized for its long, creeping stems with leaves that resemble a coin, hence the name 'pennywort'. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to improve cognitive function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Hydrocotyle laxiflora include enhanced memory, improved skin health, and reduced stress levels. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including capsules, teas, and extracts, which are commonly used to support overall health and well-being.
